description: Complete Knowledge Base & User Guide for Doable.net — a multi-location SaaS platform for service-based businesses.

articles.title: What is Doable.net?
articles.content: Doable.net is a comprehensive SaaS platform designed for multi-location service businesses (dance studios, fitness, wellness, etc.). It manages the full client lifecycle — from first inquiry as a Lead, through scheduling, enrollment, billing, and ongoing client relationship management.

articles.title: Navigation Overview
articles.content: The top navigation bar contains: Calendar | Messages | Customers | Billing | Leads | Events | E-Commerce | Reports. On the right side: shopping cart, notifications bell, and Settings gear icon (⚙).

articles.title: Key System Concepts
articles.content: Doable.net is organized in a hierarchy: Corporation (top-level) → Location (branch/studio) → User (staff) → Client. Settings flow downward — anything configured at the Corporation level automatically applies to all Locations beneath it.
rules: A Corporation is the top-level entity. All settings inherit down to Locations.
A Location is a physical branch under a Corporation.
Users can be assigned to multiple Locations within one Corporation, but not across Corporations.
Clients have a Primary Location (home location) and optional Preferred Locations.
Deactivating a Corporation prevents all users from accessing its locations.

articles.title: User Roles
articles.content: Roles are global (not location-specific). A user's role applies the same way at every location they are assigned to.

roles.name: Super Admin
roles.description: Full access across all corporations and all locations.

roles.name: Manager
roles.description: Location management, reporting, lead deletion, event management.

roles.name: Supervisor
roles.description: Elevated staff role — can create/edit events and leads.

roles.name: Counselor
roles.description: Client-facing role — can create/edit events and leads.

roles.name: Service Provider
roles.description: Frontline staff — can view calendar and manage clients but cannot drag-and-drop, delete leads, or create events.

articles.title: Settings Overview
articles.content: Settings are accessed via the gear icon (⚙) in the top navigation. The Settings area is divided into five tabs: Operations | Services | Other | Communications | Logs.

articles.title: Corporations
articles.purpose: Corporations are the top-level organizational unit. All configuration settings cascade down to Locations.
rules: Corporation Name and Time Zone are required.
Deactivating a Corp immediately prevents all users from logging into any of its locations.
All settings configured at the Corp level inherit to all Locations.
A user cannot be assigned to more than one Corporation.
A user CAN be assigned to multiple Locations within the same Corporation.
how_to_create: Enter Corporation Name (required) and Time Zone (required)
Complete optional fields: address, phone, logo
how_to_deactivate: Confirm — all location access is immediately suspended

articles.title: Locations
articles.purpose: Locations represent individual physical branches or studios under a Corporation.

subsections.name: Location Hours Setup
subsections.description: Set open/close times for each day of the week. Mark days as Closed.

holiday_blocking.description: Block specific dates to prevent scheduling at that location.
holiday_blocking.effect: When a holiday is blocked, no appointments can be scheduled on that date at this location.

subsections.name: Customer Permissions
subsections.description: Controls what clients can see and do in the client portal. Configurable per location.

subsections.name: Payment & Register Setup
subsections.description: Connect location to Doable.net payment processing. Configure accepted payment methods and POS settings.

subsections.name: Billing & Card on File
subsections.description: Configure billing rules for the location and card-on-file settings for client autopay.

subsections.name: Concierge (AI Chatbot) Setup
subsections.description: The Concierge is an AI chatbot embedded on the location's website.
features: Answers FAQs using configured question/answer pairs
Presents package information pulled from Services → Packages
Schedules FIRST appointments only (controls data entry quality)
Does NOT write data directly — guides clients through a controlled flow
how_to_configure: Navigate to Settings → Operations → Locations → Concierge section
Add FAQ entries: enter question and answer
Enable package visibility in Services → Packages (Concierge toggle)

subsections.name: Location FAQ Fields
subsections.description: Add question/answer pairs for the Concierge to reference when clients ask questions on the website.

how_to_add: Set Location Hours for each day
Configure Billing & Card on File settings
Add Concierge FAQs if using the chatbot feature

articles.title: Users (Staff)
articles.purpose: Users are staff members who access and operate the Doable.net system.

subsections.name: User Pay Setup
subsections.description: Configure pay rates — hourly, commission, or combination. Credit to staff CAN be edited after creation.

subsections.name: User Service Hours
subsections.description: Set the days and times the user is available to provide services. Controls when they appear as available on the scheduling calendar.

rules: A user's Role is global — it applies the same way at every location they are assigned to.
Users cannot be assigned to more than one Corporation.
Users CAN be assigned to multiple Locations within the same Corporation.
Service hours determine calendar availability — if not set, user won't appear as bookable.

articles.title: Automations & Follow-Ups
articles.purpose: Automations send automated notifications to clients and/or staff based on specific trigger conditions.
rules: Each automation requires its own trigger — triggers are not shared.
Automations stop when the trigger condition is resolved OR when the selected number of alerts has been sent.
No escalation rules currently, except that a Manager can be included as a notification recipient.
Automations for Leads are currently being programmed (AI Chatbot, Concierge, and Automations will be integrated).
Multiple recipients can be selected per automation.
how_to_create: Select notification channel(s): SMS / Email / In-App
Select recipient(s): Client / Last Provider / Provider on Enrollment / Manager
Set the number of alerts to send

articles.title: Scheduling Codes
articles.purpose: Color-coded labels used on the calendar to visually identify the type of service being performed. Internal use only — clients do not see them.
rules: Scheduling Codes are for calendar display only.
They do not affect pricing or enrollment.
Each service can be associated with a scheduling code.
Codes help staff quickly identify appointment types at a glance.
how_to_add: Navigate to Settings → Services → Scheduling Codes

articles.title: Services
articles.purpose: Services are the items sold to clients. They represent the actual offerings of the business.

service_classes.name: Sundry
service_classes.description: Non-appointment items (merchandise, fees). Does NOT appear on the calendar.

rules: Services are the building blocks for Packages and Enrollments.
A service must be active to be available for scheduling or enrollment.
Services can be associated with Document Library contracts.
Sundry items do not create schedulable appointments.

articles.title: Packages
articles.purpose: Packages bundle one or more services together for sale to clients.
rules: Packages can include multiple services.
Packages marked as visible to the Concierge will be presented to website visitors by the AI chatbot.
Packages are the primary enrollment vehicle — clients are enrolled into packages.
Pricing discounts can be applied at the package level.
Expiration can be set by date or by number of sessions.
how_to_add: Set Total Price and any discounts

articles.title: Document Library
articles.purpose: Stores contracts, enrollment agreements, and terms & conditions documents. Different contracts can be created for different services or packages.
rules: Documents can be associated with specific services or packages.
When a client enrolls, the associated document is presented for signature.
Documents support electronic signature.
Signed copies are retained on the client profile.
Documents can be previewed before sending.
Different services/packages can have different contracts.
Additional documents can be uploaded directly to a client profile.
how_to_add: Navigate to Settings → Services → Document Library
Upload or create the document content
Associate with Service(s) or Package(s) as needed

articles.title: Gift Certificates
articles.purpose: Allow clients or prospects to purchase a dollar-value credit applicable toward services or packages.
rules: Gift Certificates are dollar-value credits (not service-specific).
They can be applied toward any service or package purchase.
Expiration dates can be set.
Gift Certificates are tracked in the system.
how_to_create: Navigate to Settings → Other → Gift Certificates

articles.title: Event Types
articles.purpose: Categorize Events on the calendar to help staff and clients identify the nature of multi-day or special calendar entries.
rules: Event Types are used to classify Events.
An Event can be associated with multiple Event Types.
Event Types appear on the calendar to help staff and clients understand what is happening.

articles.title: Inquiry Methods
articles.purpose: Track how a Lead or new client first heard about or contacted the business. Used for marketing analysis.
rules: Inquiry Methods are selected when creating a Lead.
They help track marketing effectiveness.
Data can be used to understand which channels bring in the most leads.

articles.title: Lead Status
articles.purpose: Define the stages a prospective client moves through in the sales pipeline.

statuses.name: Scheduled
statuses.description: Lead has scheduled a first appointment.

statuses.name: Timed Out
statuses.description: All contact attempts made with no response.

statuses.name: Inactive
statuses.description: Manually discontinued contact; used for marketing specials.

rules: Status changes are manual (staff-driven).
Multiple staff can work the same lead — leads are not assigned.
Lead deletion is permanent and can only be done by a Manager.
When 'Create Customer' is selected, all lead information transfers to the client profile.
The system alerts to duplicate entries.

articles.title: Tags
articles.purpose: Custom labels applied to client profiles for segmentation, filtering, and targeted communication.
rules: Tags are created in Settings → Other → Tags.
Tags are applied to individual client profiles.
Tags can be used to filter the Student Mailing List for targeted outreach.
Multiple tags can be applied to one client.
Tags are used in the Concierge and Automation workflows (coming soon).

articles.title: Email Templates
articles.purpose: Pre-built email messages used for automated and manual communications with clients and leads.
rules: Templates are created and managed in Settings → Communications → Email Templates.
Templates can be used for automated notifications, appointment reminders, and marketing.
The Email Template list shows all available templates with their names and status.
how_to_add: Navigate to Settings → Communications → Email Templates

articles.title: SMS / Messages
articles.purpose: The Messages section (top navigation) shows SMS communication logs between staff and clients.
features: All SMS messages sent through the system are logged.
Logs show sender, recipient, message content, and timestamp.
SMS is the primary current notification channel for Automations.

articles.title: Calendar Overview
articles.purpose: The central scheduling hub for all appointments and events.
views: List view — appointments in list format

features.name: Staff Selector
features.description: Filter the calendar by one or more staff members.

features.name: Hover over appointment
features.description: Shows quick summary: client name, service, time, provider.

features.name: Drag and drop
features.description: Available for most roles. Service Providers cannot drag and drop.

features.name: Events on calendar
features.description: Appear as multi-day blocks on the right side below times at top of column.

articles.title: Creating an Appointment
steps: Click on the desired time slot on the calendar

notes.primary_location: Home location — required for scheduling and reporting.
notes.preferred_location: Additional locations allowed to see and schedule this client.
notes.special_dates: Any type — birthdays, anniversaries, etc. Will be programmed into automations for reminders.

partner_and_family.description: Partners and Family members can be linked to a client profile.

articles.title: Client Appointments
articles.description: View and manage all appointments for a specific client.

articles.title: Client Enrollments
articles.purpose: Enrollments represent a client's purchase and participation in a Package or Service agreement.

statuses.name: Active
statuses.description: Enrollment is current and in use.

statuses.name: Inactive
statuses.description: Enrollment has been cancelled. System balances credit/refund or amount due.

statuses.name: Completed
statuses.description: All sessions used OR expiration date reached. System automatically balances and closes.

rules: When an enrollment is completed (all sessions used or expiration met), it automatically moves to Completed and the system balances itself.
Cancelling an enrollment (moving to Inactive) triggers the system to calculate any credit, refund, or remaining balance due.
Only credit to staff can be edited after an enrollment is created — the agreement terms to the client cannot be changed.
Enrollment agreements (contracts) are presented for signature at time of enrollment.

articles.title: Client Payments
articles.description: Track all payments made by a client.

articles.title: Client Wallet
articles.purpose: Holds credit balances for a client that can be applied to outstanding balances.
rules: Applying wallet credit to an outstanding balance is a manual process.
Staff must manually select the credit from the wallet and apply it to the available outstanding balance.
Wallet credits can come from refunds, overpayments, or gift certificates.

articles.title: Client Card on File
articles.purpose: Stores a client's payment card for future charges.
rules: Card on file is managed from the Client Profile → Card on File tab.
Used for autopay and quick payment processing.

articles.title: Leads Overview
articles.purpose: Tracks prospective clients from first inquiry through contact attempts, conversion to customer, or closure.

articles.title: Creating a Lead
notes: The system alerts to duplicate entries automatically.

articles.title: Working a Lead
rules: Multiple staff can work the same lead — leads are not assigned to one person.
All roles can create and edit leads.
Contact attempts are logged manually on each lead after communication.
Each follow-up and connected notes are kept in a log on the client profile after conversion.
Most lead functions are available on mobile, absent calendar drag and drop.

follow_up_system.description: Follow-Up Date allows searching for leads by when staff planned to contact them.
follow_up_system.future: Plan to integrate follow-up dates to calendar alerts (coming soon).

articles.title: AI Call Feature
articles.description: Triggers an automated AI phone call to the prospect.
articles.how_to_trigger: Select the phone icon on the leads list
behavior: AI calls the prospect and offers a special
Attempts to schedule a first appointment
If the client schedules, the lead automatically moves to Scheduled status

articles.title: Converting a Lead to a Customer
steps: Staff communicates with the lead and they decide to schedule their first appointment
Staff selects 'Create Customer' on the lead record
All information entered on the lead record transfers automatically to the new client profile
Staff then schedules the first appointment
rules: All roles have the ability to convert a lead to a customer.
The system does NOT automatically convert — it is a manual staff action (will be automated soon).
All entered lead information moves to the client profile upon conversion.
The system alerts to duplicates before conversion.

articles.title: Lead Deletion
rules: Lead deletion is permanent.
Only Managers can delete leads.

articles.title: Events Overview
articles.purpose: Specialized calendar entries used to promote and communicate services, activities, and offerings that span multiple days. Visible to both staff and clients.
characteristics: Can be associated with enrollments for attendance tracking
Appear on the calendar on the right side below the times at the top of the column

articles.title: Creating an Event
steps: Select Event Type(s) — can select multiple

articles.title: Event Rules & Permissions
rules: Events appear on the calendar as multi-day blocks.
Client enrollment to events creates an enrollment record to track attendance.
Clients will soon be able to enroll themselves from the client login portal.
Event status is automatic — based on end date.
Events are not currently integrated with calendar drag-and-drop (coming soon).

articles.title: Reports Overview

output_options.name: Export
output_options.description: Send to parent company via API (manual, after accuracy check).

output_options.name: Generate PDF
output_options.description: Create PDF for printing or emailing.

access_rules: Super Admin: access across all locations and all corporations.
All other roles: restricted to their assigned location.
Currently all users can access reports, but access can be restricted by Role.
articles.important: Reports are NOT retained in the system — they must be generated and saved each time.

articles.title: Weekly Reports

extra_reports.name: Extra Reports
contains: Semi Annual Student Inventory Report (for parent company — custom)

electronic_miscellaneous_report.name: Electronic Miscellaneous Report
electronic_miscellaneous_report.description: Aggregates all clients enrolled on an event. Shows total enrollment, amount paid, remaining balances. Parent company can apply deductions for transportation and package costs.
electronic_miscellaneous_report.warning: Deduction is automatically created upon submission.

notes: Weekly Reports are reserved and custom based on Franchisor requirements or corporate requirements.
Export sends through API to parent entities manually after checking for accuracy.

articles.title: Service Provider Reports
reports: Lessons Taught by Service Provider Report
Services of Lessons Taught by Service Provider Report
filters: Select one or multiple service providers

articles.title: Student Mailing List
articles.purpose: A filterable list of all clients for marketing and communication purposes.

articles.title: Total Open Liability
articles.purpose: Shows outstanding liability owed TO clients (credit balances the business owes to clients).

articles.title: Sales Report
articles.purpose: Tracks sales activity and revenue.

articles.title: Billing Overview
articles.purpose: Manages financial transactions, payment processing, and account management.
key_functions: Process enrollment cancellations (system auto-balances credit/refund or amount due)

steps.action: Set up Payment/Register for the Location
Configure Customer Permissions for the client portal
Add Users (staff) and assign to Location(s)
Set User Service Hours for each staff member
Test the system with a sample client and appointment

workflows.name: New Client Enrollment
steps: Present and obtain signature on enrollment agreement

workflows.name: Lead to Client Conversion
steps: Lead enters system (via website, walk-in, AI call, etc.)
Staff contacts lead and logs communication
Lead decides to schedule → Staff selects 'Create Customer'
All lead info transfers to client profile

workflows.name: Running a Report
steps: Select the report tab (Weekly, Business, Service Provider, etc.)
Select the specific report from the dropdown
Click View, Generate PDF, Generate Excel, or Export as needed
Save the output (reports are not retained in the system)

workflows.name: Processing an Enrollment Cancellation
steps: System automatically calculates credit, refund, or remaining balance due
Apply any wallet credit manually if applicable

terms.definition: A rule-based notification system that sends SMS/Email/In-App messages to clients or staff when specific trigger conditions are met.
The AI chatbot embedded on a location's website. It answers FAQs, presents package information, and schedules first appointments.
The top-level organizational entity in Doable.net. All settings cascade down to Locations.
A client's active agreement to participate in a Package or Service. Includes payment terms, session counts, and associated contracts.
A multi-day calendar entry used to promote and communicate special services or activities.
A prospective client who has expressed interest but has not yet become an active customer.
A physical branch or studio operating under a Corporation.
A bundled offering of one or more services sold to clients, with pricing, expiration, and enrollment terms.
A client's home location. Used for scheduling and reporting.
Additional locations that can see and schedule a client beyond their Primary Location.
A color-coded calendar label used internally to identify service types on the calendar.
An individual offering (lesson, class, assessment, etc.) that can be scheduled and sold.
A non-appointment service item (merchandise, fees) that does not appear on the calendar.
A custom label applied to client profiles for segmentation and filtering.
A client's credit balance within the system, applied manually to outstanding balances.

upcoming_features: Lead Automations (AI Chatbot, Concierge, and Automations integration)
Client self-enrollment from client login portal
Calendar integration for Lead Follow-Up dates
